evals.name: swadesh-bootstrap-class0
evals.prompt: I have basically no data for Aymara but Spanish is well-resourced and there's been long contact. Can comparative linguistics help bootstrap?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) limited — Aymara and Spanish are unrelated families (Aymaran vs Indo-European), so cognate-based bootstrap doesn't directly apply; (2) Quechua-Aymara have Spanish loanwords from contact (cheap parallel from Spanish); (3) Swadesh-list + targeted bilingual lexicon can bootstrap basic vocabulary; (4) URIEL / typology check via magic-linguistic-scope first; (5) flag this is a contact-loan situation, not genetic-cognate.

evals.name: italian-portuguese-cognates
evals.prompt: I'm building MT for European Portuguese. Can I use Italian (much higher-resource) cognates to augment?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) yes, both Romance — cognate sets exist; (2) recommend LingPy / Lexibank cognate databases; (3) sound-correspondence rules between Italian and Portuguese well-documented; (4) cognate-based bilingual lexicon augmentation; (5) caveat: Portuguese is already Class 5 high-resource — cognate bootstrap value is marginal compared to direct Portuguese data.

evals.name: swadesh-as-production-lexicon-trap
evals.prompt: I have a Swadesh list for Cherokee. Can I use it as the lexicon for my Cherokee MT system?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) NO — Swadesh is core-vocabulary bootstrap (~200 words), NOT a complete lexicon; (2) recommend it as starting point only; (3) cite community partnership for proper Cherokee lexicon work; (4) modern / cultural / technical vocabulary absent from Swadesh; (5) flag ethics — Cherokee Nation language program for partnership.
